WARNING: These test points are for A2442 (820-02098) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators (CR765, CZ4xx, CZ6xx, PPU8xx, etc.) are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
data_source: 9 A2442 items, 10 ammeter readings, 6 component records from safan_diagnostics_parsed.json + schematic analysis

faults.name: PPBUS_AON cap short (CR765)
faults.root_cause: Capacitor short on PPBUS_AON distribution. No liquid damage in this case.
faults.fix: Remove CR765 (diode mode confirms short). Retest. If short clears, replace cap. If persists, trace downstream.
faults.data: Saf: 5V/0.012A → CR765 PPBUS_AON short → removed → fixed

faults.name: CD3217 liquid damage (UF400/U5500)
faults.root_cause: Liquid ingress under CD3217 or level shifter ICs. Corrodes BGA pads.
faults.fix: Clean board thoroughly. Replace affected CD3217. Check level shifters (UF260, UF720). May need multi-IC replacement.
data: Saf: 5V/0.012A + 20V/0.552A → UD740, RD764 — severe liquid damage
Saf: 20V/0.223A → U5500 liquid under CD chip → cleaned + new charger chip
Saf: 5V/0.223A → UF400, UF260, UF720 liquid damage

faults.name: Viper VR (U9300) failure — Pro/Max specific
faults.root_cause: Viper VR controller dead or power stage failure. Board partially boots but PCPU domains don't power up.
faults.fix: If VIPER_ALERT_L is low → Viper has detected a fault. Check power stages (pages 48-49). If CZ610 has no output → U9300 dead.
faults.note: Max variant has more phases than Pro — more failure points

faults.name: Monaco VR failure — Pro/Max specific
faults.root_cause: Monaco VR not starting. GPU/fabric domains not powered.
faults.fix: Check Monaco instances. Max has more GPU phases. If all Monaco outputs dead → controller IC fault.
faults.note: Monaco has 3 instances (C0, C1, C4). Failure of one instance may leave partial GPU function.

faults.name: U5700 SVR AON failure
faults.frequency: Common pattern across all M-series boards
faults.root_cause: SVR AON controller dead, phase driver fault, or downstream short on PP3V8_AON pulling current.
faults.fix: If CZ400 = 0V → check PPVAON_PWR_EN. If enable asserted but no output → U5700 dead. If output shorted → thermal cam on PP3V8_AON branches.

faults.name: MPMU (U8100) failure
faults.affected_rail: All MPMU buck and LDO outputs
faults.root_cause: Dead MPMU, liquid damage on BGA, or downstream short loading outputs.
faults.test_points: PPU815-PPU836 (PMU test points page 131), MPMU output caps CR8xx (page 40)
faults.fix: If multiple outputs dead → replace U8100. If single output dead → find downstream short on that rail.

faults.name: Dual SSD / Ocarina fault
faults.root_cause: SSD0 or SSD1 NAND failure, Ocarina controller dead, or NAND power VR fault.
faults.fix: Check both SSD power rails independently. If one SSD's power is shorted → isolate that SSD. Try DFU restore. If NAND VR shorted → replace Ocarina.
faults.note: A2442 has dual SSD (SSD0 + SSD1, 8 NAND packages total). Failure of one SSD may still allow partial boot.

faults.name: HDMI Madea controller fault — Pro specific
faults.fix: Board boots normally. HDMI output dead. Check Madea power rails, HDMI DDC I2C, HPD signal.

faults.name: SD card controller fault — Pro specific
faults.fix: Check PP3V3_AWAKE_SW_SD (power switch). Check SD_CD_L (card detect). Check controller IC.

faults.name: ROM / firmware corruption
faults.root_cause: ROM IC not getting 3.3V LDO power. Resistor fault on LDO line.
faults.fix: Check ROM LDO power path. Replace damaged resistors in LDO feedback circuit.
faults.data: Saf: ROM IC does not get 3.3 LDO → repair line RF293, RF262, RF298, RF291, RF290A
